title: Octucor

The '''octucor''' was a [[Forest/Legends|forest]]-dwelling land [[Squid/Legends|squid]] from the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. Sporting an eight-[[Meter/Legends|meter]]-long body with striped skin and eight three-meter-long [[Limb/Legends|tentacles]], it was a fierce [[Local day/Legends|nocturnal]] [[Diet/Legends|predator]] that reached into the [[Plant/Legends|branches]] of Endor's [[Tree/Legends|trees]] in search of prey, which included the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]]. During the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], a team of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] operatives encountered an octucor in the Endor forest that attempted to consume them.

==Biology and appearance==
The octucor was a land [[Squid/Legends|squid]] that lived on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]<ref name="Polyhedron" /> of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of the [[Endor (planet)/Legends|same name]] that was located in the [[Moddell sector/Legends|Moddell sector]], a [[Sector/Legends|subsector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Zuma sector/Legends|Zuma sector]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> It was four [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall, eight meters long, and had an elongated body with striped skin that featured a [[Mouth/Legends|maw]] and two large [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] with vertically slitted pupils. The octucor had four three-meter-long [[Limb/Legends|tentacles]] on each of the two sides of its body, with the tentacles having suckers and having broadened tips. Despite its size, the creature could move fast but also with stealth and silence, practically gliding along the [[Forest/Legends|forest]] floor with undulating movements that made it resemble a giant [[Snake/Legends|snake]].<ref name="Polyhedron" />

==Behavior==
The octucor was a nightmarish [[Local day/Legends|nocturnal]] [[Diet/Legends|predator]] that relentlessly prowled the Endor forest floor, probing the lower [[Tree/Legends|tree]] [[Plant/Legends|branches]] with its tentacles in search of prey such as the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]],<ref name="Polyhedron" /> a [[Species/Legends|species]] of meter-high tree-dwelling [[Bear/Legends|ursine]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentients]].<ref name="TEA" /> Any prey that the octucor could reach and wrap in its tentacles it then dragged into its maw and consumed, while it could also attempt to reach lifeforms sheltered in trees by bashing against their trunks to dislodge them.<ref name="Polyhedron" />

==History==
{{RPG|"[[The New Republic Campaign: The Battle for Endor from the PCs' Perspective]]"}}
At one point during the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] [[Battle of Endor/Legends|mission]] to Endor<ref name="Polyhedron" /> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="Date">{{PolyhedronCite|90|The New Republic Campaign: The Battle for Endor from the PCs' Perspective}} is set during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].</ref> a team of Rebel operatives became separated from the rest of the Alliance force sent to the moon. After nightfall, the team encountered an octucor that attempted to devour them, with the terrifying experience continuing to haunt the Rebels on the following day.<ref name="Polyhedron" />
{{Endgame}}

In another instance, a group of [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]], accompanied by several [[Weapon/Legends|armed]] Ewoks, fought three octucors in the Endor forest.<ref name="Polyhedron" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Polyhedron90-Cover.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The octucor was introduced in ''Polyhedron'' 90.]]
The octucor appeared in "[[The New Republic Campaign: The Battle for Endor from the PCs' Perspective]]," a [[roleplaying game]] source article authored by [[Bill Slavicsek]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. It was published in the [[Polyhedron 90|ninetieth issue]] of the ''[[Polyhedron]]'' magazine in [[December]] [[1993]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Polyhedron" /> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref>

The adventure suggests that the octucor be used as the most challenging encounter the player characters have to deal with during the night they spend on Endor. It also notes that the player characters can either meet the creature while still on the forest floor or—potentially following the advice of a friendly Ewok—climb a tree to spend the night in, from which they can then spot the octucor's approach before it proceeds to attack them. This article assumes the scenario plays out as described.<ref name="Polyhedron" />
